concepts.title: Definition of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: Inverse trigonometric functions are the inverse of the trigonometric functions. They give the angle whose trigonometric ratio is given. For example, the inverse sine function gives the angle whose sine is given.

concepts.title: Range of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: The range of inverse trigonometric functions is restricted to a particular interval to make them one-to-one functions. For example, the range of inverse sine function is [-π/2, π/2].

concepts.title: Properties of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: Inverse trigonometric functions satisfy the following properties: (i) sin(sin^(-1)x) = x, -1 ≤ x ≤ 1 (ii) cos(cos^(-1)x) = x, |x| ≤ 1 (iii) tan(tan^(-1)x) = x, x ∈ ℝ (iv) sin^(-1)x + cos^(-1)x = π/2, -1 ≤ x ≤ 1

concepts.title: Recalling Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'SOH-CAH-TOA' to recall the inverse trigonometric functions: sin^(-1)x → SOH, cos^(-1)x → CAH, tan^(-1)x → TOA

concepts.title: Domain and Range of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: Students often forget that the domain and range of inverse trigonometric functions are restricted to specific intervals. Make sure to remember the correct domain and range for each function.

concepts.title: Units of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: Inverse trigonometric functions are measured in radians. Remember that 180° = π radians and use this conversion factor to convert between degrees and radians.

concepts.title: Graphs of Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: The graphs of inverse trigonometric functions are reflections of the graphs of the corresponding trigonometric functions in the line y = x. This can be used to determine the domain and range of the inverse functions.

concepts.title: Equations Involving Inverse Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: To solve equations involving inverse trigonometric functions, simplify the equation and isolate the inverse function. Then, use the properties of inverse functions to find the solution.
